title: Treuten Teradoc

{{Quote|What Empire? ''We'' are the Empire.|Treuten Teradoc|<ref name="Darksaber" />}}
'''Treuten Teradoc''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] that came to power after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] collapsed. Teradoc was [[Imperial officer/Legends|officer]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] who established himself after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. After [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Death/Legends|death]] at Endor, the Empire became directionless, and with no orders coming from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Teradoc claimed dominion over the [[Greater Maldrood]], a region of territory surrounding his headquarters on [[Centares/Legends|Centares]] and the [[Maldrood sector/Legends|Maldrood sector]] where he had been stationed. The [[Er'Kit/Legends|Er'Kit]] native Teradoc secured a number of key resource [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] around his new kingdom, but his proximity to the breakaway empire of [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Zsinj]] brought him into a rivalry with his fellow warlord. Border skirmishes with Zsinj and checks on expansion by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] kept Teradoc occupied over the next several years, and the New Republic's expansion into the Empire left the warlord isolated. For a brief [[Time/Legends|time]], Teradoc provided assistance to the former steward of the Empire [[Ysanne Isard]], following her downfall. Teradoc eventually combined his Mid Rim holdings with the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] territory ruled by his brother [[Kosh Teradoc|Kosh]] into what they called the [[Federated Teradoc Union]].

When Zsinj was killed at the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Dathomir]], Teradoc moved to acquire his former worlds, but soon found himself caught in a [[Post–Zsinj campaigns|war]] with [[Teren Rogriss]] of the Empire and [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] of the New Republic. New Republic forces closed in on his territory and Teradoc was forced to flee into the Deep Core and reestablish himself there with his massive military. After ignoring the efforts of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] to reunite Imperial factions, Teradoc was drawn in by a new power rising on [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]—the [[Cloning/Legends|reborn]] Emperor Palpatine, acting in secret. With Teradoc and his fellow warlords and Imperial factions, the reunited Empire under Palpatine waged an extremely successful war against the New Republic, lasting until Palpatine's final death and the [[destruction of Byss]]. Teradoc, in control of the largest military of the warlords, returned to his kingdom, and was able to recruit [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] to lead his [[Crimson Command]]. Teradoc was soon drawn into conflict with the Deep Core warlord [[Blitzer Harrsk]], who was making moves to increase his own power. Teradoc and Harrsk's private war was brought to a halt by the maneuverings of Admiral [[Natasi Daala]], who joined with Pellaeon to force the warlords to come to the negotiation table. Daala summoned Teradoc, Harrsk, and eleven other of the most powerful Deep Core warlords to a conference at [[Tsoss Beacon/Legends|Tsoss Beacon]]. When they were unable to come to an agreement, Daala had all thirteen warlords, including Teradoc, killed.

==Biography==
===Warlord of the Greater Maldrood===
Born into a lower-class family on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Er'Kit/Legends|Er'Kit]], Treuten Teradoc had a rival almost from birth in his younger brother, [[Kosh Teradoc|Kosh]]. Although they rarely got along, the Teradoc brothers both always dreamed of action and adventure—to make these dreams a reality, they both signed up for the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. The Teradoc brothers both studied at the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Corulag Academy|Academy]] at [[Corulag/Legends|Corulag]], where they distinguished themselves as naval theorists. Fiercely competing with one another at every turn, the Teradoc brothers both gained the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]] at about the same time, and were assigned to [[Crimson Command]]—a rapid-response taskforce active in the [[Quelii Oversector]] comprised of one hundred [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyers]] with distinctive red [[Havod/Legends|havod]] alloy [[Hull/Legends|hulls]]. The sibling rivalry soon took a different turn, however, when Treuten Teradoc was promoted to [[High Admiral]] over his brother Kosh by [[Zsinj]], a development that Treuten never failed to rub in his brother's face.<ref name="warlords" />

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Teradoc was stationed in the [[Maldrood sector/Legends|Maldrood sector]]. When [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]] in the battle, the Empire became rudderless, and immediately after, Admiral [[Blitzer Harrsk]] took his [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] into the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]] to establish an independent [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] kingdom. With no orders coming from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], Teradoc followed suit only days later.<ref name="NEC" /> Teradoc set his sights on the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], offering several Imperial worlds protection from his Crimson Command and adding their planetary [[Militia/Legends|militias]] to his growing forces. After placing [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Ambris Selit]] under house arrest, Teradoc carved out his own kingdom<ref name="warlords" /> that he titled the [[Greater Maldrood]], and established his headquarters and capital on the planet [[Centares/Legends|Centares]]. Styling himself [[High Admiral]], Teradoc moved up and down the [[Perlemian Trade Route/Legends|Perlemian Trade Route]] to secure more [[Star system/Legends|systems]] for himself, taking the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyards]] at [[Abhean/Legends|Abhean]] and [[Thanos]], the industrial planets of [[Belderone/Legends|Belderone]], [[Charros IV/Legends|Charros IV]], [[Metalorn/Legends|Metalorn]], and [[Roche asteroid field|Roche]], the trade center of [[Bimmisaari/Legends|Bimmisaari]], and the critical [[Agriworld/Legends|agriworld]] [[Balamak/Legends|Balamak]]. With a wealth of resources under his control, Teradoc was able to keep his empire self-sufficient.<ref name="NEC" /><ref name="TEA" />

Teradoc wasted no time getting comfortable, growing fat in his ship's galley and generally avoiding any kind of combat. Teradoc scored a major coup when he stole the immense treasure stockpile of [[Scardia Station|Space Station Scardia]]—Teradoc soon began to style himself as a swashbuckling rogue, wielding a [[cutlass]] as he paced his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the ''[[13X]]''.<ref name="warlords" /> Though he remained nominally loyal to the Empire for the time being,<ref name="TBW">''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]''</ref> Teradoc promised protection under his aegis to the wealthier citizens of the Empire, undermining the authority of the head of the Empire at the time, [[Sate Pestage/Legends|Sate Pestage]].<ref name="Masquerade">[[X-Wing Rogue Squadron 28|''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'' 28]]</ref> After Pestage's downfall, former Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Leonia Tavira]] joined Teradoc, and from him obtained the ''[[Invidious]]'', an [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] that had been with his fleet since Endor.<ref name="IJ">''[[I, Jedi]]''</ref>

While he was able to thrive within his own [[Realspace/Legends|space]], Teradoc was distracted by his hatred of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] and the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], whose [[Hutt Space/Legends|territory]] lay bordering his own. Furthermore, he found himself in a rivalry with [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Zsinj]], who had established his own warlord kingdom to the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] north of the Maldrood. The two engaged in a number of border skirmishes over the years. Teradoc's border with Zsinj also fell prey to the [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]], who sought payback for Imperial treatment of their [[Species/Legends|species]]. In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], Teradoc's ambitions were checked when New Republic forces from [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] secured a costly [[Battle of Togoria (Galactic Civil War)|victory]] for themselves at [[Togoria/Legends|Togoria]]. By [[6 ABY/Legends|the next year]], the New Republic had advanced far enough throughout Imperial space that Teradoc and his kingdom had become isolated.<ref name="TEA" /> When the New Republic [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|took]] Coruscant that year, Teradoc dropped his pretense of loyalty to the Empire.<ref name="TBW" />

With the fall of Coruscant, the steward of the Empire, [[Ysanne Isard]], established herself as a warlord on the planet [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], home of the [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartel]]. In exchange for [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]], Teradoc traded matériel to replace her losses in fighting the New Republic. Teradoc also leased the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]] ''[[Aggregator]]'', part of his anti-[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Task force/Legends|task force]], to Isard for a high cost. He did not have much faith in her, however, and to conserve his personnel Teradoc refused to let the ''Aggregator''{{'s}} [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] complement be flown by his own people. Isard chose to use her own [[Thyferran Home Defense Corps]] instead, but they were unable to stand up to the New Republic's opposition, and the ''Aggregator'' was [[Second ambush in the Graveyard|ambushed]] by [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] in the [[Alderaan system/Legends|Alderaan system]] and driven to retreat. To discourage Teradoc from loaning any more forces to Isard, the Rogues floated the rumor that they would capture the next [[Starship/Legends|ship]] he sent. Scornful at Isard for damaging his ship, Teradoc withdrew the ''Aggregator'' from her service.<ref name="TBW" />

In [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], Teradoc ran afoul of not only Zsinj, but of the New Republic. In serious trouble, Teradoc had to call to his brother Kosh, who had carved out his own empire in the [[Deep Core/Legends|Deep Core]], for aid. Kosh came to Treuten's rescue, and together the two combined their holdings into what they called the [[Federated Teradoc Union]]. The two brothers generally got along under this arrangement, although they briefly came into conflict when Leonia Tavira played the two smitten brothers against one another.<ref name="warlords" /> When Zsinj was killed in the [[Battle of Dathomir (Hunt for Zsinj)|Battle of Dathomir]], Teradoc declared himself "High Admiral of the Mid Rim"<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|III|231|Teradoc, High Admiral}}</ref> and moved quickly across the Perlemian to secure the worlds of the [[Gordian Reach/Legends|Gordian Reach]]. However, he found himself in the middle of a [[Post–Zsinj campaigns|war]] between Admirals [[Teren Rogriss]] of the Empire and [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Gial Ackbar]] of the New Republic. Teradoc [[Battle of Junction|beat]] Rogriss at [[Feriae Junction/Legends|Feriae Junction]], winning him the allegiance of a number of Zsinj's ships, but Ackbar in turn soundly [[Battle of Maridun (8 ABY)|defeated]] Teradoc at [[Maridun/Legends|Maridun]] and [[Battle of Thanos|Thanos]].<ref name="TEA" /> Teradoc's hit and run attacks made him a nuisance to the other combatants,<ref name="NEC" /> but with New Republic forces closing on his territory on all sides, he was forced to abandon the Maldrood and flee to the Deep Core world of [[Hakassi]], where he used his massive fleet to establish himself as a power in the region.<ref name="TEA" />

===The Deep Core===
[[File:GiladPellaeon.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Gilad Pellaeon]]
In [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] united several factions of warlords behind him to wage war on the New Republic, but Teradoc and his fellow Deep Core warlords did not join the cause. In the wake of Thrawn's death, however, Teradoc was one of the first to be approached by agents of a new power broker on the Deep Core world of [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]—it was, in fact, Emperor Palpatine, resurrected into a new [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body and operating in secret. Teradoc answered the call of the Emperor, and the disparate factions of the Empire once more reunited under a common banner. Together they waged war on the New Republic, striking from the Deep Core to take Coruscant and other key [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="NEC" /><ref name="TEA" /> Teradoc fared poorly in this conflict, and at one point was ordered back to the Deep Core by his brother, who attempted to hold the Greater Maldrood himself.<ref name="warlords" /> After Palpatine was [[Battle of Onderon (Galactic Civil War)|defeated]] on [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], dying his final death, and Byss was [[Destruction of Byss|destroyed]], [[Vice Admiral/Legends|Vice Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]], another who had fought alongside Palpatine, joined with Teradoc, and the warlord put him in charge of Crimson Command.<ref name="NEC" /><ref name="TEA" /> In [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]], former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hand]] [[Roganda Ismaren/Legends|Roganda Ismaren]] attempted to use the [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]] ship ''[[Eye of Palpatine]]'' as leverage to gain influence with Teradoc and the other warlords. She was not successful, as the ''Eye'' was destroyed.<ref name="COTJ">[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']]</ref> At the same time, fellow warlord [[Blitzer Harrsk]] was attempting to gain a foothold in the [[Atravis sector/Legends|Atravis sector]]; this power-grab brought Teradoc into open conflict with Harrsk.<ref name="TEA" />

From his fortress, built into an [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] in the ring system of a lavender-and-white [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]], Teradoc sent a fleet of seventy-three ''Victories'' against Harrsk's stronghold. They destroyed Harrsk's [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Shockwave (Imperial-class)|''Shockwave'']], and crippled three more of his ships, but [[Battle of Harrsk's fortress|the attack]] provoked Harrsk to retaliate. Soon a counter-force headed by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Natasi Daala]] arrived in Teradoc's own system. Though the speed of their return left Teradoc unprepared for [[Battle of Teradoc's fortress|attack]], he had traps laid out in advance. As Daala and Harrsk's fleet moved into the rings, [[Proximity mine/Legends|proximity mines]] planted by Teradoc in the larger asteroids detonated, destroying one of Harrsk's ships and crippling two others. As Harrsk's fleet came in sight of Teradoc's asteroid fortress, Teradoc scrambled his fleet of ''Victories'' under Pellaeon's command. Teradoc observed the battle from deep within his asteroid bunker, while his fortress sent a salvo of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] fire against the intruders. However, Daala's vessel [[Firestorm (Imperial-class)|''Firestorm'']] did not engage his fleet, and soon she fired her [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannon]] upon Harrsk's own [[Whirlwind (Imperial-class)|''Whirlwind'']], immobilizing him temporarily. Daala shortly sent a communication to all the mustered forces, denouncing Teradoc and Harrsk's war with each other. She then set her ship to [[Self-destruct mechanism/Legends|self-destruct]] within fifteen [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]], promising that it would kill Harrsk unless he ceased all hostilities on his part.<ref name="Darksaber" />

Aboard his flagship ''13X'', Pellaeon issued a open-channel ceasefire for his own side. Teradoc contacted him in a rage, ordering Pellaeon to destroy Harrsk while he was vulnerable. Pellaeon refused, wanting to negotiate with Daala, and Teradoc denounced him as a traitor. Pellaeon met with Daala anyway, and Harrsk eventually gave the ceasefire order for his forces. The two [[Commander/Legends|commanders]] left the system to discuss matters, trusting that Teradoc would not open fire on Harrsk while they were gone. They eventually called a meeting of the warlords—Teradoc, Harrsk, and eleven more of the strongest rulers of the Deep Core—at [[Tsoss Beacon/Legends|Tsoss Beacon]], a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] deep in the center of the galaxy. Daala allowed only the individual warlords to approach the station, insisting they leave their fleets outside the system; Teradoc and the warlords were loath to accept these terms, and the talks were delayed two days until they all finally agreed. Eventually, they met in a conference room within the station, Teradoc being the first to arrive.<ref name="Darksaber" />

[[File:Harder_to_Breathe_TERC.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Daala executes Teradoc and other warlords at Tsoss Beacon.]]

Daala spoke of Imperial unity, pleading the warlords to join together to fight their common enemy, which Teradoc dismissed as a platitude. [[Superior General]] [[Sander Delvardus]] took his contempt of the proceedings a step further, attempting to leave, at which point Daala sealed all the doors with a [[cyberlock]] that would not open for three [[Standard hour/Legends|hours]]. She gave the warlords that much time to choose a leader for themselves. For hours, they argued with little progress, until tensions built to the point that Harrsk leapt at Teradoc and began choking him. Pellaeon hauled the other warlord off Teradoc, but at this point Daala had had enough. She castigated the warlords for their failure to unite, and then, as she and Pellaeon put on [[Breath mask/Legends|breath masks]], she flooded the room with [[Nerve toxin/Legends|nerve gas]]. Teradoc was the first to stand and attempt an escape, but it was fruitless. One by one the warlords succumbed to the gas and fell. Teradoc was the last to die, a fact which surprised Daala, given his obesity.<ref name="Darksaber" />

==Personality and traits==
From his youth, Treuten Teradoc was driven by his intense rivalry with his brother Kosh. Although the two had a rather cold relationship, as children they both idolized the legendary swashbuckler Piethet Brighteyes, whom Treuten later styled himself after as the warlord of the Greater Maldrood. The sibling rivalry pushed the Teradoc brothers to great heights in the Imperial Navy, and Treuten was always apt to rub in any advantage that he had over his younger brother.<ref name="warlords" /> Teradoc was an obese man, often red-faced and sweaty and moving with difficulty. His strategic philosophy was to build up a fleet of smaller, yet more versatile ships, such as the ''Victory''-class Star Destroyer. He preferred to command from a safe location deep within his fortress, while his ships formed a more disposable defense around him. Teradoc's own subordinate, Gilad Pellaeon, held the man in contempt for this, and considered him far beneath one of the [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]] he had served, Grand Admiral Thrawn.<ref name="Darksaber" /> Nonetheless, Pellaeon learned the value of psychological warfare, including terror strikes, in part from Teradoc.<ref name="NEC" /> For his own part, Teradoc—a firm believer in loyalty to one's [[Commanding officer/Legends|commanding officer]]—considered Pellaeon a traitor after the man's alliance with Natasi Daala.<ref name="Darksaber" /> Teradoc was diligent in his duty and extremely anti-Rebel;<ref name="TBW" /> during Operation Shadow Hand he rallied to the Emperor's call,<ref name="TEA" /> but after Palpatine's final death Teradoc dismissed any Imperial government outside himself and the warlord kingdoms. He considered notions of Imperial unity to be mere platitudes.<ref name="Darksaber" />

==Behind the scenes==
While Teradoc made his first appearance in [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s [[Darksaber (novel)|''Darksaber'']], he actually received brief mention in the novel set and published immediately before it, [[Children of the Jedi (novel)|''Children of the Jedi'']]. Though Teradoc was established as being in the Deep Core there, the novel [[X-Wing: The Bacta War|''The Bacta War'']], set earlier, placed his operations in the galactic Rim, and later works like [[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge|''Isard's Revenge'']] and [[X-Wing: Starfighters of Adumar|''Starfighters of Adumar'']] indicated his involvement in actions near the [[Ciutric Hegemony]] and the domain of Zsinj, also in the outer reaches of the galaxy. [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] continued to claim Teradoc's only holdings were in the Deep Core, not explaining the discrepancy; this was resolved at last in [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', which, in addition to finally giving Teradoc a first name, added to his backstory a time when his domain lay in the Mid Rim, with his time in the Deep Core pushed to the later part of his career. The [[2013]] [[StarWars.com]] blog post ''[[The Imperial Warlords: Despoilers of an Empire]]'' gave a large amount of information on Teradoc's backstory, including his homeworld and his rivalry with his brother Kosh.

Yet, there was still the discrepancy, that ''The Essential Atlas'' claims, Treuten Teradoc became a warlord in the aftermath of the Battle of Endor, while the Teradoc mentioned in ''The Bacta War'' was said to have become a warlord after the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|New Republic conquest]] of [[Coruscant]]. In [[2020]], [[Leland Chee]] confirmed that the Teradoc that appeared in the fourth book of the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (novel series)|X-Wing]] novel series, [[X-Wing: The Bacta War]], was in fact Kosh Teradoc, not Treuten.<ref name="Twitter">{{Twitter|HolocronKeeper|status/1320771770605998082|[[Leland Chee]]|quote="''But, if Treuten became a warlord after Endor, and the Teradoc from Bacta War after Coruscant, does that mean the Teradoc from the 4th X-Wing novel (who also lend Isard a ship), is actually Kosh? /3''" "That was my understanding."|archivefile=File:Leland_Chee_Teradocs.png}}</ref>
